template_name: Standard Prescription Format - Murmur in a Neonate

condition.name: Murmur in a Neonate
overview: A murmur in a neonate is not always pathological; it may be due to transitional circulation or a hemodynamically insignificant lesion.
A murmur may be the only sign of critical congenital heart disease (CCHD).
The aim is to distinguish innocent from pathological murmurs and identify babies needing urgent referral or echocardiography.

opd_importance: Most murmurs are detected during routine newborn or early infant examination.
Early detection of duct-dependent lesions can be lifesaving.
Clinical examination should be integrated with pulse oximetry screening.

description: May change with position or feeding

history_points: Prenatal scan findings and any known cardiac anomaly
Antenatal risk factors: maternal diabetes, infections, drug exposure
Family history of congenital heart disease
Feeding difficulty or diaphoresis during feeds
examination_points: Type, timing and location of murmur
Check for tachypnea, hepatomegaly, gallop rhythm
Measure four-limb blood pressure if needed
Look for dysmorphism such as Down syndrome or Turner syndrome
Assess feeding pattern and weight gain
red_flags: Murmur in first 24 hours of life
More than 3% saturation difference between right hand and foot
Blood pressure gradient suggestive of coarctation

investigations.name: ECG
investigations.purpose: Supportive test; may be nonspecific in neonates

if_pathological_murmur_suspected: Admit if unstable, cyanotic or tachypneic
Consider prostaglandin infusion if critical congenital heart disease is suspected and lesion is duct-dependent

parent_counseling_points: Not all murmurs are dangerous; some resolve spontaneously. — सभी दिल की आवाज़ें खतरनाक नहीं होतीं; कुछ अपने आप ठीक हो जाती हैं।
If echocardiography is advised, it is to ensure the baby's heart is normal. — अगर इको टेस्ट बोला गया है तो यह बच्चे के दिल की जांच के लिए है।
Explain warning signs: cyanosis, breathing difficulty, poor feeding. — खतरे के निशान: नीलापन, सांस में तकलीफ, दूध न पीना।
Encourage regular follow-up even after a normal echocardiogram. — सामान्य रिपोर्ट आने पर भी नियमित जांच कराएं।

saturations.pathological: Below 95% or more than 3% pre/post ductal gradient

flow_logic: Assess symptoms, oxygen saturation and femoral pulses
If well-appearing with soft systolic murmur of grade 2/6 or less and normal saturation/pulses, observe and review
If symptomatic, loud, diastolic, persistent or associated with abnormal saturation/pulses, suspect CCHD
Perform pulse oximetry and echocardiography and refer to pediatric cardiology
quick_checklist_for_opd: Is it grade 2/6 or less and systolic only?
Is there no cyanosis and is the baby feeding well?
Are femoral pulses palpable and symmetric?
If yes, observe and review; if no, arrange echo and referral.

standard_prescription_format.title: Standard Prescription - Murmur in a Neonate
provisional_diagnosis_options: Neonatal murmur - rule out congenital heart disease

if_pathological_suspected: Consider prostaglandin infusion if duct-dependent lesion suspected

advice_to_parents: Watch for poor feeding, bluish discoloration, fast breathing, excessive sweating during feeds.
Return immediately if any warning signs appear.
Keep follow-up even if baby seems well.

warning_signs: Blue discoloration of lips or tongue — होंठ या जीभ का नीला पड़ना
Fast or difficulty breathing — तेज़ या मुश्किल सांस लेना
Poor feeding or excessive sweating during feeds — दूध न पीना या दूध पीते समय बहुत पसीना आना
Lethargy or reduced activity — सुस्ती या कम हिलना-डुलना
Cold hands and feet — हाथ-पैर ठंडे पड़ना
expected_course: Innocent murmurs usually resolve within the first few months of life as transitional circulation stabilizes. Pathological murmurs require echocardiography and may need ongoing cardiology follow-up or intervention.
key_clinical_points: Pulse oximetry screening (pre- and post-ductal) is mandatory for all neonates with a murmur.
Femoral pulse assessment is critical to rule out coarctation of aorta.
A grade >=3/6, harsh, diastolic, or pansystolic murmur should be considered pathological until proven otherwise.
Echocardiography is the gold standard investigation.
Do not delay referral if critical congenital heart disease (CCHD) is suspected.

severity_assessment.mild: Soft systolic murmur (grade 1-2/6), asymptomatic, normal saturations and femoral pulses — observe and review.
severity_assessment.moderate: Murmur persisting beyond 48 hours, any associated symptoms, or saturation concern — arrange echocardiography.
severity_assessment.severe: Loud/harsh murmur, cyanosis, desaturation, absent femoral pulses, poor feeding — urgent cardiology referral and consider CCHD.
